gomodel:run-figure1-opus48/tph1/enabled_.snippet: tph-1 resolved to WB:WBGene00006600; serotonin-biosynthetic enzyme in ADF neuron (figure NEURONS region).

gomodel:run-figure1-opus48/mod1/enabled_.snippet: mod-1 resolved to WB:WBGene00003386; serotonin-gated chloride channel in URX neuron (figure NEURONS region).

gomodel:run-figure1-opus48/ser6/enabled_.snippet: ser-6 resolved to WB:WBGene00021897; octopamine GPCR in AWB neuron (figure NEURONS region).

gomodel:run-figure1-opus48/tbh1/enabled_.snippet: tbh-1 resolved to WB:WBGene00006541; octopamine-biosynthetic enzyme in RIC neuron (figure NEURONS region).

gomodel:run-figure1-opus48/nhr76/enabled.snippet: nhr-76 resolved to WB:WBGene00015497; intestinal nuclear hormone receptor / transcription factor (figure INTESTINE box).

gomodel:run-figure1-opus48/atgl1/enabled.snippet: ATGL/atgl-1 resolved to WB:WBGene00015484; intestinal triacylglycerol lipase (figure INTESTINE box).

gomodel:run-figure1-opus48/tph1/molecula.snippet: tph-1 annotated to tryptophan 5-monooxygenase activity (IBA), the rate-limiting enzyme of serotonin biosynthesis in the ADF neuron.

gomodel:run-figure1-opus48/tph1/part_of.snippet: tph-1 annotated to serotonin biosynthetic process (IBA); produces the 5-HT signal shown in the ADF->URX arrow.

gomodel:run-figure1-opus48/tph1/occurs_i.snippet: tph-1 annotated to neuron projection (IBA); figure places tph-1 in the ADF neuron of the NEURONS region (no resolvable WBbt CURIE available, GO CC used).

gomodel:run-figure1-opus48/mod1/molecula.snippet: mod-1 annotated to serotonin-gated chloride channel activity (IDA); receives the 5-HT signal in URX.

gomodel:run-figure1-opus48/mod1/part_of.snippet: mod-1 annotated to serotonin receptor signaling pathway (IDA/IMP); transduces the ADF-derived serotonin signal in URX.

gomodel:run-figure1-opus48/mod1/occurs_i.snippet: mod-1 annotated to plasma membrane (IDA/EXP); figure places mod-1 in the URX neuron of the NEURONS region.

gomodel:run-figure1-opus48/ser6/molecula.snippet: ser-6 annotated to octopamine receptor activity (IDA); receives the octopamine (Oct) signal from RIC/tbh-1 in AWB.

gomodel:run-figure1-opus48/ser6/part_of.snippet: ser-6 annotated to octopamine signaling pathway (IDA); transduces the Oct signal in AWB.

gomodel:run-figure1-opus48/ser6/occurs_i.snippet: ser-6 annotated to plasma membrane (IBA); figure places ser-6 in the AWB neuron of the NEURONS region.

gomodel:run-figure1-opus48/tbh1/molecula.snippet: tbh-1 annotated to tyramine-beta hydroxylase activity (IMP); final enzyme producing octopamine in the RIC neuron.

gomodel:run-figure1-opus48/tbh1/part_of.snippet: tbh-1 annotated to octopamine biosynthetic process (IMP); produces the Oct signal shown in the RIC->AWB arrow.

gomodel:run-figure1-opus48/tbh1/occurs_i.snippet: tbh-1 annotated to synapse (IDA); figure places tbh-1 in the RIC neuron of the NEURONS region.

gomodel:run-figure1-opus48/nhr76/molecul.snippet: nhr-76 annotated to nuclear receptor activity (IBA); ligand-activated transcription factor in the intestine.

gomodel:run-figure1-opus48/nhr76/part_of.snippet: nhr-76 annotated to positive regulation of transcription by RNA polymerase II (IMP); figure shows nhr-76 driving transcription of atgl-1 in the intestine.

gomodel:run-figure1-opus48/nhr76/occurs_.snippet: nhr-76 annotated to nucleus (IEA/IBA); figure places nhr-76 in the INTESTINE box.

gomodel:run-figure1-opus48/atgl1/molecul.snippet: atgl-1 annotated to triacylglycerol lipase activity (ISS/IBA); intestinal lipase mobilizing stored fat.

gomodel:run-figure1-opus48/atgl1/part_of.snippet: atgl-1 annotated to triglyceride catabolic process (IBA); captures the figure's ATGL->beta-oxidation/Fat loss arrow (lipolysis releasing fatty acids that feed beta-oxidation), modeled as the lipase's BP slot rather than a causal edge to a downstream gene.

gomodel:run-figure1-opus48/atgl1/occurs_.snippet: atgl-1 annotated to lipid droplet (IDA/EXP); figure places ATGL in the INTESTINE box.

gomodel:run-figure1-opus48/tph1/causal/g.snippet: tph-1 (ADF) serotonin biosynthesis is causally upstream, positive effect, of mod-1 serotonin-gated channel activity in URX, via the diffusible 5-HT intermediate (figure Panel E ADF->URX arrow labeled '5-HT'). Noble, Stieglitz & Srinivasan 2013 describe an integrated serotonin circuit where ADF-derived serotonin acts on MOD-1 to control body fat.

gomodel:run-figure1-opus48/tbh1/causal/g.snippet: tbh-1 (RIC) octopamine biosynthesis is causally upstream, positive effect, of ser-6 octopamine receptor activity in AWB, via the diffusible octopamine (Oct) intermediate (figure Panel E RIC->AWB arrow labeled 'Oct'). The 2013 Noble et al. integrated serotonin/octopamine circuit places octopamine from RIC acting on its receptor.

tbh-1 (RIC) octopamine signaling indirectly positively regulates mod-1 channel activity in URX (figure Panel E dashed arrow RIC->URX, indicating an indirect connection). The integrated octopamine/serotonin circuit (Noble et al. 2013) converges on URX/MOD-1.

gomodel:run-figure1-opus48/ser6/causal/g.snippet: ser-6 (AWB) octopamine signaling causally upstream, positive effect, of tph-1 (ADF) serotonin biosynthesis (figure Panel E AWB->ADF arrow; LOW CONFIDENCE/UNCONFIRMED - verify).
gomodel:run-figure1-opus48/ser6/causal/g.justification: Figure Panel E shows a solid pointed arrow from AWB (ser-6) to ADF (tph-1), but the curator flagged this edge as low-confidence/unconfirmed (0.3) and verification against the figure was inconclusive. No specific publication for a ser-6 -> tph-1 positive regulation was found via Europe PMC or Alliance interactions. Included for figure fidelity and tagged instinct so the curator verifies the arrow's existence and direction.

gomodel:run-figure1-opus48/mod1/causal/g.snippet: Compartment-level NEURONS->INTESTINE 'endocrine signal' edge (figure Panel E bold dashed arrow from URX hexagon crossing into the INTESTINE box). Modeled as the most-downstream neuronal activity (mod-1/URX) indirectly positively regulating the most-upstream intestinal activity (nhr-76), since the signal travels via the circulatory system (endocrine) to a distant tissue. Noble, Stieglitz & Srinivasan 2013 show the neuronal serotonin/octopamine circuit directs release of an endocrine signal to control intestinal body fat.

gomodel:run-figure1-opus48/nhr76/causal/.snippet: nhr-76 nuclear-receptor/transcription-factor activity indirectly positively regulates atgl-1 triacylglycerol lipase activity via transcriptional activation of atgl-1 (figure Panel E nhr-76->ATGL arrow); gene expression is the intervening module, hence indirect.
gomodel:run-figure1-opus48/nhr76/causal/.justification: Figure Panel E shows a solid pointed arrow from nhr-76 to ATGL within the INTESTINE box; process hint states nhr-76 mediates transcription of atgl-1. As a transcription factor regulating its target's gene expression, the relation is indirect (gene expression is the intervening module). No specific PMID for nhr-76 -> atgl-1 was found via Europe PMC or Alliance interactions, so tagged instinct for curator verification. nhr-76's own positive-regulation-of-transcription role and atgl-1's lipase role are independently GO-annotated.
